content:
# Revelation Chapter 14 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE, pages 287-288 | **Source Image:** REV_014_page_001.jpg, REV_014_page_002.jpg

1. And I saw, and behold, the Lamb standing upon the Mount Sion, and with him a hundred [and] forty-four thousand having the name of him and the name of the Father of him written upon the foreheads of them.

2. And I heard a voice out of the heaven as [the] voice of many waters and as [the] voice of great thunder. And the voice which I heard [was] as [that of] harp-players harping upon the harps of them.

3. And they sing a new song before the throne and before the four living creatures and the elders. And no one was able to learn the song except the hundred [and] forty-four thousand, the [ones] having been purchased from the earth.

4. These are the [ones] who with women were not defiled, for they are virgins. These [are] the [ones] following the Lamb wherever he goes. These were purchased from the people, a firstfruit to the God and to the Lamb.

5. And in the mouth of them was not found falsehood, for they are blameless.

6. And I saw another angel flying in mid-heaven, having [the] eternal good news to proclaim upon the [ones] sitting upon the earth and upon every nation and tribe and tongue and people,

7. saying in a great voice, Fear the God and give to him glory, because the hour of the judgment of him has come, and worship the [one] having made the heaven and the earth and [the] sea and [the] springs of waters.

8. And another angel, a second, followed, saying, It fell, it fell, Babylon the great, who from the wine of the passion of the fornication of her has caused all the nations to drink.

9. And another angel, a third, followed them, saying in a great voice, If anyone worships the beast and the image of it and receives a mark upon the forehead of him or upon the hand of him,

10. he also will drink of the wine of the wrath of the God, the [wine] having been mixed undiluted in the cup of the anger of him, and he will be tormented in fire and sulphur before [the] holy angels and before the Lamb.

11. And the smoke of the torment of them goes up unto ages of ages. And they do not have rest day and night, the [ones] worshipping the beast and the image of it, and if anyone receives the mark of the name of it.

12. Here is the endurance of the holy [ones], the [ones] keeping the commandments of the God and the faith of Yeshua.

13. And I heard a voice out of the heaven saying, Write: Blessed [are] the dead, the [ones] in [the] Lord dying from now. Yes, says the Spirit, that they will rest from the labors of them, for the works of them follow with them.

14. And I saw, and behold, a white cloud, and upon the cloud [one] sitting like [a] son of man, having upon the head of him a golden crown and in the hand of him a sharp sickle.

15. And another angel came out of the temple, crying in a great voice to the [one] sitting upon the cloud, Send the sickle of you and reap, because the hour to reap has come, because the harvest of the earth has dried.

16. And the [one] sitting upon the cloud cast the sickle of him upon the earth, and the earth was reaped.

17. And another angel came out of the temple, the [one] in the heaven, he also having a sharp sickle.

18. And another angel came out of the altar, the [one] having authority over the fire, and he called with a great cry to the [one] having the sharp sickle, saying, Send the sharp sickle of you and gather the clusters of the vine of the earth, because the grapes of it have ripened.

19. And the angel cast the sickle of him to the earth and gathered the vine of the earth and cast [it] into the winepress of the wrath of the God, the great [one].

20. And the winepress was trodden outside the city, and blood came out of the winepress up to the bridles of the horses, from [a distance of] a thousand six hundred stadia.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **14:1 — "the name of him and the name of the Father of him":** Sinaiticus reads the longer form specifying both names. Some manuscripts read simply "his name and the name of his Father." The Sinaiticus phrasing is preserved literally.

2. **14:4 — "from the people" (απο των ανθρωπων):** Sinaiticus reads "from the people/mankind" rather than the variant "from the earth" found in some witnesses. This is a notable Sinaiticus reading.

3. **14:5 — "falsehood" (ψευδος):** Sinaiticus reads ψευδος ("falsehood/lie"). Some later manuscripts add δολος ("deceit/guile"). The shorter Sinaiticus reading is followed.

4. **14:8 — "It fell, it fell":** The doubled aorist εpesen εpesen is preserved, reflecting the prophetic announcement form. Sinaiticus includes the repetition clearly.

5. **14:12 — "the faith of Yeshua":** The genitive construction πιστιν Ιησου could be objective ("faith in Jesus") or subjective ("the faithfulness of Jesus"). EMET preserves the literal genitive "faith of Yeshua" without interpretive smoothing.

6. **14:15 — "has dried" (εξηρανθη):** Sinaiticus uses the aorist passive of ξηραινω, literally "was dried" or "has dried." This is more vivid than the common translation "is ripe" — the harvest is pictured as dried and ready for cutting.

7. **14:20 — "a thousand six hundred stadia":** This is approximately 184 miles / 296 kilometers. The number is preserved literally as read from the manuscript.

— EMET
